title: Wireless adaptor and wireless gateway having built-in firewalls for secure access to instruments
abstract: An apparatus and method is disclosed for the secure access to field instruments. An interface device that includes a built-in firewall, is communicatively coupled between the device manager of an industrial automation process control system and a network of field instruments. The interface device includes at least one processor configured to execute instructions that provides a firewall for the one or more field instruments by blocking one or more user selected commands from being sent to the field instruments from the device manager.
claims:
Claims WHAT IS CLAIMED IS: 1. An apparatus comprising: a wireless adaptor (118, 418) comprising at least one interface (206) configured to be coupled to one or more wired field devices (420a-420c); the wireless adaptor (118, 418) also comprising at least one wireless radio configured to communicate over a wireless network and to receive commands for the one or more wired field devices (420a-420b) over the wireless network; the wireless adaptor (118, 418) further comprising at least one processor (202) configured to execute instructions for providing a firewall for the one or more wired field devices; and wherein the at least one processor (202) is further configured to receive information from a user (303) identifying one or more configuration rules (405) for the firewall that block one or more of the commands from being sent to the one or more wired field devices (420a-420b). 2. The apparatus of Claim 1, wherein: the at least one interface (206) is configured to be coupled to one or more wired Highway Addressable Remote Transducer (HART) field devices; and the at least one wireless radio is configured to use a WirelessHART wireless network protocol. 3. The apparatus of Claim 1, wherein the one or more configuration rules (405) identify that one of: all universal and common practice write commands are blocked; certain universal and common practice write commands are blocked; and certain vendor-specific commands are blocked. 4. A method comprising: coupling at least one interface (206) of a wireless adaptor (118, 418) to one or more wired field devices (420a-420c); using at least one wireless radio of the wireless adaptor (118, 418) to communicate over a wireless network and to receive commands for the one or more wired field devices (420a-420c) over the wireless network; using at least one processor (202) of the wireless adaptor to execute instructions 

 for providing a firewall for the one or more wired field devices; and receiving information from a user (303) identifying one or more configuration rules (405) for the firewall for blocking one or more of the commands from being sent to the one or more wired field devices (420a-420c). 5. The method of Claim 4, wherein: the at least one interface (206) is coupled to one or more wired Highway Addressable Remote Transducer (HART) field devices; and the at least one wireless radio uses a WirelessHART wireless network protocol. 6. The method of Claim 4, wherein the one or more configuration rules (405) identify that one of: all universal and common practice write commands are blocked; certain universal and common practice write commands are blocked; and certain vendor-specific commands are blocked. 7. An apparatus comprising: a wireless gateway (112, 312) comprising at least one interface (206) configured to be coupled to one or more networks; the wireless gateway (1 12, 312) also comprising at least one wireless radio configured to communicate over a wireless network and to transmit commands for one or more wireless field devices (320a-320c) over the wireless network; the wireless gateway (112, 312) further comprising at least one processor (202) configured to execute instructions that provides a firewall for the one or more wireless field devices (320a-320c); and wherein the at least one processor (202) is further configured to receive information from a user (303) identifying one or more configuration rules (305) for the firewall for blocking one or more of the commands from being sent to the one or more wireless field devices (320a-320c). 8. The apparatus of Claim 7, wherein the at least one wireless radio is configured to use a Wireless Highway Addressable Remote Transducer (WirelessHART) wireless network protocol. 9. The apparatus of Claim 7, wherein the one or more configuration rules (305) identify that one of: all universal and common practice write commands are blocked; certain universal and common practice write commands are blocked; and certain vendor-specific commands are blocked. 10. A method comprising: coupling at least one interface (206) of a wireless gateway (112, 312) to one or more networks; using at least one wireless radio of the wireless gateway (112, 312) to communicate over a wireless network and to transmit commands for one or more wireless field devices (320a-320c) over the wireless network; using at least one processor (202) of the wireless gateway to execute instructions for providing a firewall; and receiving information from a user (303) identifying one or more configuration rules (305) for the firewall for blocking one or more of the commands from being sent to the one or more wireless field devices (320a-320b). 11. The method of Claim 10, wherein the at least one wireless radio uses a Wireless Highway Addressable Remote Transducer (WirelessHART) wireless network protocol. 12. The method of Claim 10, wherein the one or more configuration rules (305) identify that one of: all universal and common practice write commands are blocked; certain universal and common practice write commands are blocked; and certain vendor-specific commands are blocked. 13. The apparatus of Claim 1, wherein the wireless adaptor (118, 418, 518)) includes at least one I/O unit (208) configured to be coupled to a handheld programming device (501); the wireless adaptor (518) executing instructions that blocks one or more of the commands from being sent to the one or more wired field devices (520) from the handheld programming device (501).
